reviews.title: It was a most disappointing experience from a very loyal guest!
reviews.positives: Location - the primary reason for staying in this hotel!
reviews.negatives: I have stayed in this property 4 times in the last months. I keep returning because I enjoyed my past stays. But things have changed and the property has deteriorated in terms of service and amenities. Hotel also does not seem to give value to guests who keep returning to the property. Even simple requests are ignored. And room was not clean and electrical outlets are not working. We were very disappointed.

reviews.title: Facility is good but policy is not humble
reviews.text: We were staying for marriage proposal night, everything was good till next day morning, we tried using Nespresso and my cup was over filled with coffee by pressing its preset button. I was scald and dropped the saucer, it went broken. The hotel charged US$70 for this damage as they tried to explain how expensive the item is. I paid the damage without further complaints due to my good mood from proposal night, but I believe the Hotel valued customers less comparing to a cup in a room.  The Nespresso is not properly set and may scald others, furthermore the customer's feelings are well ignored. With great disappointment I must confess the Hotel is not as humble as it claims.

reviews.title: Rude staff
reviews.text:
I booked for 2 guest with breakfast buffet included in the cost but when I went to enjoy breakfast with my husband the staff rudely turned him away. The staff did not look into the matter for us and clarify the booking. Instead they rudely turned my husband away and made us feel unwelcomed. I ate my breakfast alone feeling so terrible first thing in the morning. It is our first trip in Taipei and we always heard about the exceptional hospitality and service but this was definitely not delivered by the staff.
It is very unfortunate and I have never experienced this before in all my hotel bookings. Please be aware before booking.

reviews.text: I have been to this hotel few years back then before COVID and everything was excellent. For the stay this time there are two main issues: staff are not patience and attitude wasn't a five star rating, secondly I have never been to a five star hotel with no catering or room service after 12am and within that center district? I was trying to get a coke and beer at midnight but the staff said the restaurant is closed and they have no such room service after restaurant closing, and asked me to order take away? I have never had such room service experience for a hotel like this with this price. What if customers are hungry at night or want something else to drink? A five star hotel should cater the needs of customer living in the room right?

Smoking ban also not enforced at the drop off which leads to an incredible stink when you are forced to enter the lobby there. I wouldn’t bring my kids there again for sure. Zero stars

reviews.title: staff and management issues
reviews.text:
I stayed here due to business travel. At check out, hotel staffs said they cannot provide receipt (with dollar amount), because I booked it from one of internet travel portals!

After lengthy discussions, when I left the hotel I still do not have any receipt at hand. I never encountered this from my many years of travel experience.

At check in, the staff said with straight face that there is no chance to check in until 3pm. Since it's so clear that under pandemic, most hotel rooms are available, I questioned another lady and see if I can check in earlier. Immediately she said yes.

Overall, very disappointed with humble hotel.

reviews.title: Not great value at all
reviews.text:
If the hotel is well located and the premises clean, the stay was an overall disappointment.  
1) The gym is actually not usable, incredibly small and disappointing.  As soon as you have two people in there, it's game over. Not that there is anything for you have a workout, though.  Apart from a single bench (not a bench press, just a bench) that seems to have been there since the foundation of Taipei, there is only a rack of dumbbells (too light for a decent workout) and no space to use them. 
2) Apart from the Italian restaurant, there are no dining options.  Said Italian has a very long queue to get in (it has to be good, then, but frankly, if you don't plan your day around your lunch, forget about it). Fortunately, there are restaurants all around in the malls.  Not really what I was expecting, but it's good to be able to eat something. 
3) The staff, though nice, is really trying hard to upsell you stuff, like upgrades.  It is clearly company policy and I don't want to criticize these guys for this, but it can be a little annoying when you are just trying to check in.  
4) The rooms at the "basic plus" level are small and the bathtubs tiny.  Really tiny.  The staff will try and sell you an upgrade for NTD 2,000 allowing you to have a better bathtub, shampoo and amenities.  Seriously?

The in-room internet works well, though.  Not amazing, but well enough.  And that's already it.  No extra charge, even.  

Altogether, my analysis is that this is not a great choice for a business traveler.  If you are looking for a place to stay without hassles, a place to get a good workout and get some business meetings in, a plug-and-play kind of thing, this is not it.  This was my first try, but I will not go back. There are loads of both better and cheaper options around in Taipei.

reviews.title: Nice hotel but disappointing service and details
reviews.text: I stayed for 3 nights and loved the room and the convenient location to shopping and the MRT. I was disappointed by the low language level of the team. I know English is not their first language, but when you pay the rates of Humble House, I expect not to have to explain myself multiple times. I have lived and worked in Asia for 12 years and am use to a communication issues but did not expect this here. I also found that the a Emeritis in the room were a little disappointing. The body wash was not replaced even though very close to empty and infact ran out during my stay and the Nespresso only offered 2 capsules in a box built for 4. These may seem like small details but in hotels, it is the details that matter.

reviews.title: second time here
reviews.text: We first stayed at Humble House for new year’s countdown in 2019/2020 — it was such a memorable stay counting down to Taipei 101 fireworks so we really missed Humble House and specially spent 2 nights here for our wedding anniversary. This time round, while our room was on a higher floor, the Taipei 101 view wasn’t as good. And while we appreciate the nice touch with the special room towels decoration, the room doesnt come with any basic amenities at all (a very stark contrast especially as we came from Japan)! Am a little surprised as Humble House is branded as a luxury hotel after all. Tried breakfast for the first time and some offerings like the local noodles and soy milk, egg roll, are very good.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.text:
I am writing to express my serious concern regarding the handling of our credit card information during our recent stay.

We booked our reservation through Agoda, with the understanding that payment would be processed upon departure. On the second or third day of our stay, we received an email from the credit card center informing us that a partial payment had been made, which I understood, and I was aware that the remaining balance would be charged at checkout. When we checked out, the invoice appeared correct.

However, shortly after, we received another message from your hotel stating that there had been a mistake made by your staff, resulting in the cancellation of the previous charge and a recharging of the total amount. This left us with no opportunity to review the charge details or verify the original payment amount.

I am quite concerned that, having left our credit card information with your hotel, there seems to be a lack of transparency regarding charges. It feels as though your hotel can charge whatever amount it deems appropriate. As a globally recognized brand, Hilton has a reputation to uphold, and I am apprehensive about the security and trustworthiness of our credit card information with your establishment.

I hope to hear from you regarding this matter soon.

reviews.title: Great hotel rooms with excellant location
reviews.text:
The location is excellant, right above the shopping mall. Hotel is clean and bed is very comfortable. 
Staff service can be improved. I booked 4 superior rooms on Agoda and upon check in, the staff tried to sell us some upgrade deals in order to put all 4 rooms on the same floor. After a few rounds of proposal and re-proposal on upgrade pricing deal, we find it too confusing, troublesome and rejected. It was then we realised that hotel seems to have overbooked. After our rejection, the staff told us that he will "auto" upgrade all our rooms without needing us to pay! Imaging we have agreed on his initial proposal and paid for upgrade, how silly do we look? Overall check in + negotiation took us 30min..  So, try your luck.

The room is nice and clean, just the square bathtub was a bit small, so its hard for soaking. It would be great if hotel staff asked me how was my stay at the hotel upon check out. Anyways, for this price, I think I will pay a bit more and stay at Le Meridien Taipei (right beside Humble House) next time.

reviews.title: Great boutique hotel near 101 Tower
reviews.text: This is the first time I stayed at Humble House Taipei, and I think it's a great hotel at a great location. In the neighbourhood there's also W Hotel, Le Meridien, and Grand Hyatt Taipei, but this is the newest one in the bunch, and its ambience and design is low-key yet chic. The hotel is filled with amazing contemporary art pieces which makes you feel like living inside a gallery. Service is great. Hotel is within walking distance to 101 Tower (actually you can view it from your room) so it's very convenient for both business and leisure. I would love to stay at this hotel again in my next visit.

reviews.title: Slick looking but no soul
reviews.text: Humble house appears to be a nice business hotel and all quite sleek. From the lobby, all the way to your room, the interior is quite nice and refined. I booked a deluxe twin room but upgraded upon checkin to a deluxe king room, as i was staying by myself. Inside the room, all the electronics are there - curtains, lights, sheer, etc. The interior of the room is actually quite nice and sleek but that's about it. The swimming pool is ok, but for some unknown reason, they make you wear swimming caps!! I've actually stayed at the W Taipei and there's no swimming cap requirement there. Then the gym - This gym was extremely small for a business hotel. It looked like it was the last amenity to be designed for the hotel so they squished into the last empty room in the hotel. It is tiny with only several machines and one bench. This is the tiniest hotel gym I have ever seen and not worth the price paid, since the W and Le Meridian gyms are significantly bigger and well equipped. Will not stay there again as the price isn't that dissimilar from Le Meridian (W Hotel is slightly more expensive) which is actually next door to Humble House.

reviews.title: Overall good stay but customer service could be better
reviews.text:
Location was excellent, close to shopping malls and lots of F&B options nearby. Very clean room and the daily cleaning/ tidying up was top notch. Pool was closed for maintenance during our stay. Staff were polite but not the most friendly. When we checked in before 3pm, the receptionist simply said "pls leave your luggage" without any elaboration on whether the room could be ready a bit earlier. We then asked if we could potentially get the room earlier in which she simply said no. When we checked out and had to drop the luggage for storage for a few hours, the luggage drop-off desk was not staffed and when a staff eventually showed up, she simply asked "what do you want" (in mandarin). 
Overall a good hotel to stay in if you simply want to stay in a 5* hotel without the extra customer service.

reviews.title: Was given room that I wanted in the end but manager was RUDE (ran out of space to elaborate on him)
reviews.text: Everything about this hotel & stay was great except for two minor mishaps. The names & descriptions of the rooms should not have discrepancies & the manager should have a better attitude; he was very stuck up. I booked a room that was listed "Deluxe Room with Double Bed" & was given the option to choose a "large bed"/"two twin beds" at checkout. I selected the latter. I then received an e-mail from Humble House stating that I booked "Deluxe King" & was asked to amend my reservation on Agoda. I looked at my confirmation & nothing on it was related to having booked a King so I responded that there must have been an error & attached a screenshot of my reservation for them. When I arrived at the check-in, the gentleman (manager) was stuck up & had a bad attitude. I asked him about the mix-up & his response was that booking a "double" meant "king" worldwide & MADE ME FEEL DUMB. I've stayed at enough hotels & this is definitely not the case. Don't make it my problem when it was yours.

Posizione eccezionale, con la via pedonale sotto l’hotel sempre viva e piena di negozi e ristoranti. Camera moderna e confortevole, con una vista mozzafiato sul Taipei 101. 
Piscina molto bella con vista sui grattacieli. Perfetto per vivere la città.
Colazione ottima ma anche tanto locali dove mangiare nei dintorni, basta inoltrarsi a piedi nel quartiere oltre la fermata della metro Taipei City Hall (Metro Bannan Line a 300 metri).
Il rapporto qualità prezzo è ottimo vista la posizione super privilegiata. 
Lo staff è stato gentilissimo e hanno anche provveduto a tenere la valigia che non ci serviva mentre giravamo altre città taiwanesi.
Gentilissimi sempre.

reviews.title: Great Location
reviews.text: My husband and I enjoyed our stay at Humble House.  It was very clean, aesthetically pleasing with nice amenities, and best of all, so well located in the heart of the city.

reviews.negatives: La moquette in camera

reviews.title: A good experience that lives up to the Hilton name.
reviews.text:
It is a 5 to 10-minute walk from Taipei City Hall Station or Xiangshan Station.

Although it is not right next to Taipei 101 Tower, the location is appropriate for using transportation that is close enough. The view quality of the swimming pool is overwhelming. It is surprising that all staff, except for the housekeeper we did not meet, could communicate in English despite not speaking any Chinese. Regardless of how well they speak...

The fact that the breakfast menu changes slightly by day is a good point. The taste is average. While I think the price range is somewhat high, the overall facility quality and care for requests are very satisfactory. Instead of thinking "this is what Hilton is," it was a good experience that made me feel that "this is why it is Hilton." If I go to Taipei again, I would definitely consider revisiting.
